experiences.context: Consumer robotics; owns the crown module (cameras, infrared, microphones); an AI-driven sensor system in production.

bullets.text: Reduced false-positive sensor rejection rate from 75% to under 30% in three months by diagnosing root causes in production validation, unblocking manufacturing-line throughput.

Defined acceptance bounds for the crown module's sensor stack (cameras, infrared, microphones), tuning thresholds against human-interaction baselines that caught ~40% of defects before they reached assembly.
Own the crown module roadmap across hardware, firmware, and ML; shipped 3 quality gates in Q1 that cut the average defect-escape rate from assembly to field.
Aligned 3 engineering teams (hardware, firmware, ML) on shared acceptance criteria, cutting cross-team review cycles from ~2 weeks to 4 days.
Built Rust backend services replacing 5 fragmented spreadsheets with a structured data layer, giving hardware and production teams live build-state visibility across ~200 units/month.
Refactored data-collection pipelines for production-line robots, cutting memory overhead ~60% and tightening event-ordering guarantees to make anomaly traces reliable.

experiences.context: Social-impact nonprofit; consumer mobile app for non-technical volunteers.

bullets.text: Shipped end-to-end chat coordination (in-app messaging, real-time location, verification, reimbursement) on a consumer mobile app, cutting median task-completion time ~20% in the three months post-launch.

Ran 23 stakeholder interviews across volunteers, requesters, and analysts; synthesized findings into a prioritized 6-month roadmap with problem statements and success metrics driving the next 2 quarters of work.
Built the usage-data case that secured executive approval for a new initiative; segmented 1,200 active users and isolated the 2 engagement drivers responsible for ~65% of retained sessions.

Designed and shipped a serverless analytics platform on AWS (Lambda, DynamoDB, SQS, S3) that ingested high-volume consumer event streams and powered low-latency reporting APIs consumed by 3+ product teams.
Architected REST API data models and query-caching strategies that lifted throughput ~3x and cut DynamoDB read costs ~40%, letting the platform scale without capacity increases.
Owned production lifecycle for 4 Lambda services: CI/CD (GitHub Actions), CloudWatch alerting, on-call response; held 99.5% uptime over 8 months with 12 incidents resolved same-day.

experiences.context: Consumer fitness app, 10K users; on-device ML pose-correction feature.

bullets.text: Set latency target and prioritized tradeoffs for a CNN pose-estimation model, partnering with ML on distillation and quantization that took inference from 490ms to 67ms (~7x) and cut per-inference cost ~28%.

Designed in-app feedback mechanisms (comments, star ratings) prototyped in Figma to capture implicit usage signals, driving an estimated ~25% lift in monthly sensor-engaged sessions over the following quarter.
Wrote the PRD for a CNN-based pose-estimation feature on a fitness platform with ~10K registered users, defining edge cases and success metrics through user research with 30+ athletes.
Owned requirements for the pose-correction feature, sourcing 2 IMU sensor datasets (AMASS, DIP-IMU) with ~15K body-keypoint sequences for model training.
Triaged user complaints from in-app channels, identified the top 3 session drop-off points, and partnered with engineering on patches that cut mid-session abandonment ~18%.
Rebuilt the ML inference pipeline on AWS Lambda (Python), cutting p95 latency from 490ms to 67ms (~7x) through cold-start optimization, request batching, and model-loading improvements.
Shipped on-device TensorFlow Lite inference, cutting cloud inference cost 28%, lifting model accuracy 30%, and growing MAU 45%.

experiences.context: Pharma consulting; supported a sales incentive disbursement platform that allocated ~$32M annually across the client's pharma sales force.

bullets.text: Built ROI analysis showing platform optimization beat new-feature development; presented to senior leadership, driving a roadmap shift that protected ~$500K in billable time and unlocked ~$400K in new-feature revenue.

Owned the analytics for a sales incentive platform allocating ~$32M annually across the client's pharma sales force; built segmentation, payout-logic checks, and reconciliation the ops team relied on each cycle.
Owned post-launch optimization for the $32M platform, eliminating ~35% of support volume over two quarters by triaging tickets, isolating recurring UI/UX pain points, and shipping targeted fixes.
Defined and tracked platform-adoption OKRs in an Agile cadence, driving 45% active-user growth (to 580) and a 26% quarterly lift in feature delivery velocity.
Managed 3 vendor relationships (1 sales-data provider, 2 external partners), coordinating delivery timelines that held 100% on-time data delivery for 4 consecutive quarters.
Standardized onboarding workflows for new client cohorts, cutting average time-to-first-value from ~6 weeks to ~3 weeks and giving customer success a repeatable first-run playbook.
Built and tuned Java/SQL payment-processing services for a $32M enterprise platform, contributing ~$1.2M in incremental revenue by hardening error handling and retry logic to reduce payment failures.
Modeled domain entities and transaction workflows in SQL schemas and OOP design, reducing payment-processing bugs ~30% by cleanly separating business logic from persistence layers.

experiences.context: Solo-founded marketing agency in undergrad (sometimes branded as Kalo Labs in older resume variants). Closed to graduate.

bullets.text: Built and scaled a solo marketing agency to $4K/mo with 7 clients in undergrad through Facebook and Instagram ads, ClickFunnels sales funnels, and direct outreach, before closing it to graduate.
Owned every function end-to-end as sole operator: ad creation, sales funnels, sales calls, client delivery, and billing; averaging ~$570/client/month across 7 accounts.

projects.name: Search Engine in Rust
projects.description: Rust search engine built from scratch

bullets.text: Building a search engine from scratch in Rust, chapter-by-chapter through Manning's Introduction to Information Retrieval, targeting sub-10ms p95 query latency on 10M-document corpora.
Built a positional inverted index over 11,314 documents from 20 Newsgroups in Rust with the dictionary in RAM and postings on disk, enabling nanosecond term lookups and single-seek retrieval.
Cut query latency 7x on the first iteration by sorting query terms by posting-list length once at index-build time rather than re-sorting per query; a structural change with linear scaling benefits.
Built a three-layer spell corrector from scratch (trigram index → Jaccard filter → Levenshtein distance) that corrected 'Uniited Staates of Ameeriica' and returned phrase-verified results in 19ms.

Replaced bincode with a hand-rolled Variable Byte (VByte) encoder, shrinking index size 76% (34.8MB to 8.3MB) at the cost of 50% slower queries (444ms to 665ms); a tradeoff that breaks even at 1M docs and wins at 10M+.
Implemented TF-IDF with cosine normalization to fix length-bias in ranking, promoting a relevant 13-line post above an unrelated 1,835-line archive on the query 'United States of America.'

Built tiered indexes per Manning Ch. 7.2.1 by running a histogram over 1.7M (term, doc, tf) triples to data-drive thresholds, exposing that textbook defaults put just 0.49% of postings in Tier 1, motivating corpus tuning.

Replaced strict phrase matching with proximity scoring (boost = 1 + k/w, smallest window with all query terms); turned 0 results into 10 on 'israeli palestinian' and nearly doubled the 8802/9059 score ratio (2.3x to 4.25x).

Refactored the index-merge step from 'all blocks in RAM' to a streaming k-way merge with a per-block reader, holding constant memory regardless of corpus size and unblocking the path to million-document scale.
Building in public with 9+ articles on krithik.xyz logging each chapter's tradeoffs; every claim grounded in measurements from the 11K-doc corpus, including failures.

Implemented two-pointer merge intersection over sorted posting lists at O(x+y), turning a nested-loop O(xxy) into a linear walk; for lists of 500 and 5,000 that's 5,500 comparisons vs 2,500,000, ms vs minutes at scale.

Implemented skip pointers at √P interval and benchmarked vs two-pointer merge for posting-list intersection; at 11K-doc scale, skip-check overhead exceeded comparisons saved, so the code was deferred for larger lists.
Implemented Levenshtein edit distance via DP on an mxn cost matrix at O(mxn) per pair, ranking spell-correction candidates after a trigram + Jaccard pre-filter narrows 120K vocab terms to a handful.
Block-based index construction at 4,000 docs per block, alphabetically sorted within each block to enable streaming merge; flush, clear memory, repeat; so RAM stays bounded regardless of corpus size.

Pre-computed cosine vector lengths during the merge step; every doc's tf-idf weight squared and summed in one streaming pass; so query-time normalization is a single HashMap lookup per doc instead of O(vocab).

Fixed a ranking failure where a 1,835-line archive (doc 9059) outranked a 13-line on-topic post (doc 8802) under raw TF-IDF; identified length bias and applied cosine normalization, lifting doc 8802 from #14 to #1.

Refactored the term-index dictionary into a TermEntry struct with tiered offsets and doc_freq, threading a tier_idx through retrieval calls so one disk file holds three tier regions with HashSet dedupe across tiers.

Hand-coded a proximity boost `1 + k/w` per Manning S.7.2.2 (k = query terms, w = smallest covering window); chose multiplicative-not-additive to avoid lifting weak tf-idf docs, computing w via greedy k-pointer advance.

Grounded Heaps' law (vocabulary grows as √T) and Zipf's law in real corpus measurements: 'of' takes 394KB of postings vs 4KB for 'america', explaining why VByte gap-encoding compresses common terms 77%+.

Planned next: re-index the Cranfield benchmark (1,398 abstracts, 225 queries) and compute MAP and P@10 against qrels before and after BM25; two data points on one standard benchmark beats two ad-hoc setups.

Measured setup end-to-end: 17.8s indexing 11,314 docs + 4.9s merging 138,743 terms = 22.7s one-time; queries then run without rebuilding, scaling to tens of millions of docs at the same RAM budget via dictionary-in-RAM.

projects.name: filmsearch
architecture_notes: Multi-stage pipeline: semantic intent recognition → strict BM25 → iterative query relaxation
v2 zone schema: title, plot, actors, director (per-zone TF-IDF tables)
v2 database tables: Zone Importance (term-zone scores), TF-IDF (per-zone term frequencies), AllFilms (metadata), Zone Vectors (pre-computed for cosine similarity)
v2 cosine zone selection: top-3 zones per term based on query-vs-zone similarity
Composite score = sum of zone-importance scores per term; zero-composite terms act as stop-word filter
v1 (failed) used FastAPI for LLM endpoints, Llama 3.1 70B (Groq), 384-dim pgvector embeddings, json_repair for LLM output cleaning, 12 critical fields extracted from 95+ raw scraped fields

bullets.text: Built a semantic search engine over a 54K+ film corpus scraped from Wikipedia, answering queries like 'films about love and space' end-to-end from Scrapy to PostgreSQL/Supabase to a React/Next.js frontend on Vercel.

Implemented Okapi BM25 from scratch with multi-zone TF-IDF tables (title, plot, actors, director) and tuned hyperparameters (k1=1.5, b=0.75), improving retrieval relevance 35% over baseline string-match.

Designed query relaxation: pre-computes per-word composite importance scores and iteratively drops the least-important term when strict match returns fewer than 3 results, turning 0-result queries into useful ones.
Built a semantic pre-filter using sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 to encode queries and zone names as 384-dim vectors, routing via cosine similarity to focus BM25 on the most relevant zones.

Shipped v1 with Llama 3.1-extracted data and 384-dim embeddings; it ran but didn't work; then rebuilt v2 four weeks later by replacing the LLM-heavy approach with classical IR (BM25 + multi-zone), which worked.

Validated the rebuild when a startup founder used filmsearch to find a niche Japanese animated film he couldn't surface elsewhere; unsolicited evidence of product-market fit for descriptive discovery.

Built the data pipeline end-to-end: a Scrapy crawler for Wikipedia film articles, Pandas + NLTK preprocessing, Scikit-learn vectorization helpers, and a Node.js/Express server backing the React/Next.js client.

Documented limitations and future work openly in the README: multiple-DB-call latency, BM25 |D| approximation, and the case for a hybrid lexical+semantic single-score formula, establishing credibility through honesty.

Published a 4-minute Medium deep-dive (Aug 2025) walking readers through the BM25 formula, query relaxation logic, and semantic vector layer; supplemented with a Loom video walkthrough and YouTube intro.

Built v1 on Next.js, Node/Express, FastAPI, and Llama 3.1 70B via Groq with 384-dim pgvector embeddings, distilling 95+ scraped fields per film into 12 structured fields before pivoting to classical IR.

Designed v2 schema with four cooperating tables (Zone Importance, per-zone TF-IDF, AllFilms metadata, pre-computed Zone Vectors), routing each query to top-3 zones per term via cosine similarity for low-latency retrieval.

Ported the BM25 scoring kernel to C++ for benchmarking against the Python implementation, achieving 12x throughput on 54K documents by leveraging contiguous memory layout and compiler-optimized inner loops.

projects.name: Voyantra
projects.description: Collaborative travel planner with Amadeus API

bullets.text: Shipped Voyantra, a MERN-stack collaborative travel planner integrated with the Amadeus API for real-time flight, hotel, and rental data; 103 commits, JWT auth, Tailwind CSS; winning the Northeastern Hackathon 2024.

Designed a personalized-itinerary input flow (dates, guests, origin, destination) returning unified flight, hotel, and rental cards in one interface; replacing the multi-tab booking flow with one bookable workflow.

Built real-time collaboration where shared itineraries refresh travel options when any participant adjusts dates, with REST API contracts and a Mongo schema handling concurrent multi-user updates without merge conflicts.

Owned the full stack as solo builder; React + Tailwind CSS frontend, Express/Node backend with modular controllers, services, and routes; integrating Amadeus API, JWT auth, and document storage end-to-end.

projects.name: ChuckleBox
projects.description: AI humor highlighter on AWS SageMaker

bullets.text: Prototyped ChuckleBox on AWS SageMaker (Python) that ingests audio, transcribes, segments laughter, and surfaces punchline timestamps; won the MIT Sundai Club Hackathon in 1 day with a ~16-person team.

Built the inference pipeline end-to-end: audio chunking, speech-to-text transcription, laugh detection against trained checkpoints, timestamp emission; packaged in a Docker container with shell-script deploy.

Tested the pipeline against real recorded audio from MIT's Building E39 and shipped a live demo at chuckle-box.vercel.app; concrete proof that an end-to-end audio ML system can ship in a single hackathon day.

projects.name: AI Learning Skill
projects.description: Claude tutor skill with spaced repetition
projects.framework: Justin Skycak's Advice on Upskilling (Math Academy framework)
architecture_notes: Three modes: Diagnose (find gaps), Plan (build prereq tree), Coach (run retrieval loop)

Eight non-negotiable rules: no passive re-ingestion, prereq audit before new material, minimum effective dose of instruction, review should feel challenging, bite-sized pieces, measurable progress over feelings, no shortcut validation, top-down plan with bottom-up execution
Five grading categories: clean (double interval), clean but slow (hold interval), peeked (does not count, re-rep), failed (shrink interval), didn't attempt (send back)

Four-file architecture: SKILL.md (operating manual, read-once), OPERATING_PRINCIPLES.md (strategic, monthly), SESSION_SUMMARY.md (tactical, every session), learning_log.md (per-rep grades + due dates)
Adaptive spaced repetition: clean retrievals double the next interval (1d → 3d → 1w → 2w → 1m → 3m), fuzzy retrievals shrink it back
Flinch tracking: catalogues the user's known evasion patterns (mechanics-dump on conceptual questions, article-as-rep substitution) and pushes back when they recur

bullets.text: Built a Claude skill before opening Manning's IR textbook to fight the fluency illusion: three modes (Diagnose, Plan, Coach), eight rules, and five retrieval grades where peeked never counts as clean.

Designed an adaptive spaced-repetition ladder where clean retrievals double the interval (1d, 3d, 1w, 2w, 1m, 3m) and fuzzy retrievals shrink it, with the schedule recomputed per rep by grade rather than fixed in advance.

Built a flinch-pattern tracker that catalogs known evasion behaviors (mechanics-dumping, articles-as-reps) and pushes back when they recur, turning a passive tutor into an alignment system that fights user dodging.

Architected the skill as four layered files (read-once SKILL.md, monthly OPERATING_PRINCIPLES, per-session SESSION_SUMMARY, rep-level learning_log) so context survives between Claude sessions without drift.

Used the skill as the harness for the Rust search engine build, producing 7+ shipped articles, 12 Rust files, and a learning log of receipts where the system caught fuzzy retrievals self-grading would have missed.

education.degree: Master of Science in Engineering Management
